description: Canonical registry of every WorldMonitor-derived data subset azimuth may surface. The per-source license guardrail (scripts/check_sources.py) validates every entry before any subset is surfaced as L1. A subset that is not in this registry, or whose entry fails the guardrail, MUST NOT be surfaced. See docs/source-guardrail.md. Each surfaced source carries a `theme` that maps it to one L2 brief (see `themes` below); excluded sources carry a `surfaced_reason` explaining why they stay surfaced:false.

content_class_policy.line: fact-vs-propaganda (Michael 2026-06-24). A channel is surfaceable iff it (1) monitors FACTS — observed events, measurements, positions, records — AND (2) is free-to-use (license-clean). Sensitivity is NEVER a deny reason; only LICENSE or non-factual (opinion/propaganda) content is. A factual record is allowed even on a sensitive topic — a conflict EVENT, a ship POSITION, a flight TRACK, a power outage are observed facts; an OPINION about them is propaganda.
content_class_policy.note: DENIED classes are non-factual content: political propaganda, opinion/advocacy, editorial/communication, political-or-safety POSITION-taking, investment advice. The gate is `factual AND free-to-use license` — a restricted/commercial feed stays out on LICENSE grounds (not editorial). NEWS is allowed as a factual channel (news-factual) — the per-source filter keeps only fact-reporting wire/agency sources and strips op-ed/editorial/advocacy outlets. ALL sensitive-topic factual EVENT classes (conflict-event-record, vessel-position, flight-track, cyber-event, civil-unrest-event) are allowed once the feed license is free. `benchmark_foil_classes` (forecast / intelligence-assessment / scenario) are NOT surfaced as azimuth channels — they are predictions/assessments/hypotheticals, not observed facts; azimuth uses them as BENCHMARK foils (observed+sourced facts vs predicted/assessed opinion) per work-item azi-benchmark-vs-forecast-intelligence-W26. prediction-market raw odds stay allowed (the quoted price is an observed fact) but carry a synthesis caution forbidding investment framing in L2. `climate-observation` (added W26) is an observed measurement of the climate system — atmospheric CO2 concentration, sea-ice extent — sourced from public-domain US-gov / NSIDC instrument records; it is a recorded MEASUREMENT (a fact), never a climate FORECAST, and carries the report-observed-not-predicted caution. W26 full-universe audit (Michael 2026-06-24, docs/sources/worldmonitor-channel-audit.md): six observed-fact classes were added when the audit enumerated the full ~34-family WorldMonitor universe and surfaced every free-licensed factual channel — `sanctions-record` (an official government designation is a recorded fact, OFAC public-domain), `health-event` (a disease-outbreak record, WHO/CDC public-domain), `maritime-safety-notice` (an NGA navigational warning is an issued fact, US-Gov public-domain), `orbital-position` (a satellite TLE position, CelesTrak/US-Gov public-domain), `trade-record` (an applied tariff / trade-restriction record, WTO CC-BY), and `economic-indicator` (a published macro statistic, World Bank CC-BY). All are observed/recorded facts, never forecasts/assessments — those stay benchmark foils.

prediction-markets.title: Prediction Markets Weekly
prediction-markets.hold_reason: L1 ingest is ACTIVE (channel passes the guardrail). The quoted prediction-market PRICE is an observed fact (allowed under the 2026-06-24 fact-vs-propaganda line). The L2 brief is held for a synthesis-breadth reason only — NOT an editorial deny: the live feed currently surfaces a single market, too narrow to evolve into a balanced weekly brief without that one market dominating. Brief lands once the feed carries a broader market set. Reporting the price is fine; the hold is about brief breadth, not topic sensitivity.

environmental-hazards.title: Environmental Hazards Weekly
environmental-hazards.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): the four channels (wildfire-detections, thermal-escalations, natural-events, radiation-observations) are license-cleared (NASA FIRMS / NASA EONET+GDACS / US-EPA RadNet+Safecast — all public-domain), credited, and pass the guardrail. The L2 weekly brief lands once the curator's first scheduled pull writes the L1 notes this brief synthesises. Same surfaced:true-with-held-brief pattern as prediction-markets.

conflict-watch.title: Conflict Watch Weekly
conflict-watch.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): conflict-events-ucdp is surfaced via UCDP (Uppsala Conflict Data Program), which publishes its event data under CC-BY-4.0 — a free, attribution-only license that the azimuth CREDITS.md satisfies. A conflict EVENT record is an observed fact (allowed under the fact-vs-propaganda line; sensitivity is never a deny reason). The ACLED feed for the same topic stays held-on-license (its ToU restricts redistribution). Brief lands after first ingest.

maritime-safety.title: Maritime Safety Weekly
maritime-safety.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): maritime-navwarnings is surfaced via NGA (US National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency) Broadcast/Navigational Warnings — a US-Gov public-domain feed. A navigational warning is an issued, observed maritime-safety fact. (The separate vessel-AIS-position feed stays held-on-license: the AIS aggregator license is commercial/unknown.) Brief lands after first ingest.

cyber-watch.title: Cyber Watch Weekly
cyber-watch.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): cyber-threats is surfaced via abuse.ch (URLhaus + Feodo Tracker), whose IOC feeds are released under CC0-1.0 (public-domain dedication). A cyber-incident IOC (C2 host, malware URL) is an observed fact. Brief lands after first ingest.

sanctions-watch.title: Sanctions Watch Weekly
sanctions-watch.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): sanctions-designations is surfaced via the US Treasury OFAC SDN + Consolidated lists (sanctionslistservice.ofac.treas.gov) — US-Gov public-domain. A sanctions designation is an official, recorded government act (a fact), not a political opinion about it. Brief lands after first ingest.

public-health.title: Public Health Weekly
public-health.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): disease-outbreaks is surfaced via WHO Disease Outbreak News + US CDC Health Alert Network — both public-domain / open-data. A reported disease-outbreak event is an observed public-health fact. Brief lands after first ingest.

macro-markets.title: Macro & Markets Weekly
macro-markets.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): the three channels (crypto-quotes via CoinGecko API-ToS, world-bank-indicators via World Bank Open Data CC-BY-4.0, tariff-trends via WTO CC-BY-4.0) are license-cleared and credited. A quoted price / published indicator value / applied-tariff record is an observed fact (carries no-investment-framing caution). Brief lands after first ingest.

orbital-watch.title: Orbital Watch Weekly
orbital-watch.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): orbital-satellites is surfaced via CelesTrak TLE element sets, which derive from US Space Command public-domain orbital data. A satellite's TLE-propagated position is an observed/recorded fact. Brief lands after first ingest.

humanitarian.title: Humanitarian Watch Weekly
humanitarian.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): displacement-flows is surfaced via UNHCR Refugee Data + UN OCHA HAPI — both free-to-use/open-with-attribution (OCHA HAPI is CC-BY-4.0). A refugee/IDP population count is an observed humanitarian fact. Brief lands after first ingest.

infrastructure-watch.title: Infrastructure Watch Weekly
infrastructure-watch.hold_reason: INGEST-PENDING (not editorial): internet-outages (Cloudflare Radar, API-ToS) and chokepoint-status (IMF PortWatch, free/open) are license-cleared and credited. An internet-outage event and a chokepoint-transit count are observed infrastructure facts. Brief lands after first ingest.

sources.upstream_source: NOAA / NASA reanalysis regional temperature & precipitation anomalies
NOAA Global Monitoring Laboratory (atmospheric CO2 record)
sources.attribution: Data: NOAA Global Monitoring Laboratory via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: NSIDC / NOAA@NSIDC Sea Ice Index (satellite-passive-microwave sea-ice extent)
sources.attribution: Data: NSIDC Sea Ice Index via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.cap_reason: The live endpoint returns the FULL active-fire set (~10k+ detections / ~3MB) and ignores limit/pageSize/minFrp query params, so a verbatim daily L1 note would bloat the repo ~3MB/day. azimuth caps the L1 note to the top 250 detections by FRP (fire radiative power = the strongest, most newsworthy fires); the cap is stated in the note caption and the full set always remains at the endpoint. This is the payload-size fix that unblocked re-adding the NASA FIRMS wildfire channel (Azimuth W26-USP).

sources.upstream_source: NASA FIRMS (VIIRS thermal anomalies, clustered into escalation signals)

sources.attribution: Data: NASA EONET + GDACS via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: US EPA RadNet + Safecast (ambient radiation measurements)
sources.attribution: Data: US EPA RadNet (public domain) + Safecast (CC0) via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: UCDP (Uppsala Conflict Data Program — Georeferenced Event Dataset)
sources.attribution: Data: UCDP (Uppsala Conflict Data Program) via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: NGA (US National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency) Broadcast/Navigational Warnings
sources.attribution: Data: NGA Maritime Safety navigational warnings via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: abuse.ch (URLhaus malware URLs + Feodo Tracker C2 indicators)
sources.attribution: Data: abuse.ch (URLhaus + Feodo Tracker, CC0) via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: US Treasury OFAC SDN + Consolidated sanctions lists
sources.attribution: Data: US Treasury OFAC (sanctionslistservice.ofac.treas.gov) via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)
sources.surfaced_reason: LIVE-REACHABILITY HOLD (not editorial/license): the endpoint /api/sanctions/v1/list-sanctions-pressure returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized on the live 2026-06-25 ingest — it now requires auth beyond azimuth's free anonymous session, so it fails the Path-A free-tier bar. Re-surface when a clean free-tier endpoint is confirmed; the OFAC source is otherwise license-clean (US-Gov public domain).

sources.upstream_source: WHO Disease Outbreak News + US CDC Health Alert Network
sources.attribution: Data: WHO Disease Outbreak News + US CDC HAN via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)
Data: World Bank Open Data via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: WTO (World Trade Organization — applied tariffs & trade restrictions)
sources.surfaced_reason: LIVE-REACHABILITY HOLD (not editorial/license): the endpoint /api/trade/v1/get-tariff-trends returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized on the live 2026-06-25 ingest — now auth-gated beyond the free anonymous session, failing the free-tier bar. Re-surface when a clean free-tier endpoint is confirmed; the WTO source is otherwise CC-BY-4.0.

sources.upstream_source: CelesTrak TLE element sets (derived from US Space Command public catalog)
sources.attribution: Data: CelesTrak TLE (US Space Command public catalog) via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.upstream_source: Eurostat HICP / national statistical offices (consumer price overview)
sources.surfaced_reason: LIVE-REACHABILITY HOLD (not editorial/license): the endpoint /api/consumer_prices/v1/get-consumer-price-overview returned HTTP 404 Not Found on the live 2026-06-25 ingest — the path is not live on api.worldmonitor.app. Re-surface when a working endpoint is confirmed; the Eurostat source is otherwise CC-BY-4.0.

sources.upstream_source: UNHCR Refugee Data + UN OCHA HAPI (refugee / asylum / IDP counts)
sources.attribution: Data: UNHCR + UN OCHA HAPI via WorldMonitor (api.worldmonitor.app)

sources.surfaced_reason: LIVE-REACHABILITY HOLD (not editorial/license): the endpoint /api/supply_chain/v1/get-chokepoint-status returned HTTP 404 Not Found on the live 2026-06-25 ingest — the path is not live on api.worldmonitor.app. Re-surface when a working endpoint is confirmed; the IMF PortWatch source is otherwise API-ToS-derived.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS ONLY (re-evaluated W26 2026-06-24): a conflict EVENT record is an observed fact (allowed — sensitivity is never a deny reason). ACLED stays surfaced:false because the ACLED Terms of Use restrict redistribution of event data and require a use agreement / forbid re-serving the dataset — that exceeds azimuth's free-to-use-with-attribution bar even as a non-commercial demonstrator with CREDITS.md. The SAME conflict topic IS now surfaced via UCDP (conflict-events-ucdp), which publishes under a clean CC-BY-4.0 license. ACLED becomes surfaceable if/when a clean ACLED license path is confirmed; the hold is purely license, never editorial.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS ONLY (re-evaluated W26 2026-06-24): a vessel POSITION is an observed fact (allowed — sensitivity is never a deny reason). The vessel-AIS feed stays surfaced:false purely because the AIS-aggregator license is commercial/unknown (not in the free-to-use allowlist). The Maritime theme is NOT empty, though: maritime-navwarnings (NGA navigational warnings, US-Gov public-domain) IS now surfaced under the 'maritime-safety' theme. Vessel-AIS becomes surfaceable once a free-to-use AIS feed is confirmed; the hold is purely license, never editorial.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS ONLY: a flight TRACK is an observed fact (allowed under the 2026-06-24 fact-vs-propaganda line — sensitivity is never a deny reason). Air-traffic/ADS-B would be the 'Air-traffic' theme; it stays surfaced:false purely because the ADS-B Exchange license is restrictive (commercial; not in the free-to-use allowlist). OpenSky Network is a partial free-for-research alternative but its ToS still restricts redistribution of raw positions. Surfaceable once a clean license is confirmed; no editorial barrier remains.

sources.upstream_source: gpsjam.org (H3-gridded GPS/GNSS jamming, derived from ADS-B Exchange)
sources.surfaced_reason: HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS ONLY: a GPS-interference observation is an observed fact (factual, allowed). Held because gpsjam.org derives from ADS-B Exchange, whose license is restrictive (same upstream-license bar as military-flights-adsb). No editorial barrier — surfaceable once the upstream ADS-B license is free-to-use.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS ONLY: a quoted price is an observed fact (factual, allowed). Held because the Yahoo Finance feed is proprietary-with-fair-use, not in the free-to-use allowlist — fair-use display is not the same as a redistribution license for a derived public vault. Crypto quotes (CoinGecko, API-ToS) ARE surfaced; equities wait on a clean license.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS ONLY: a published index value is an observed fact (factual, allowed). Held because The Economist's Big Mac Index is proprietary (CC-BY-NC-SA-style restrictions on the published dataset), not in the free-to-use allowlist. No editorial barrier.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS: a recorded trade flow is an observed fact (factual, allowed). UN Comtrade is widely cited as CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO but its dissemination terms restrict bulk redistribution and the free tier is rate/volume-limited; pending an explicit confirmation it stays held. tariff-trends (WTO, CC-BY-4.0) IS surfaced as the clean trade channel. No editorial barrier.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS: a live webcam frame is an observed fact, but each stream is a third-party YouTube broadcast under its own uploader's terms — no blanket free-to-use license for re-surfacing. Low priority. No editorial barrier; held purely on per-stream licensing.

sources.upstream_source: Satellite imagery providers (mixed; Sentinel/Landsat free, commercial tiers not)
sources.surfaced_reason: HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS: imagery is observed fact, but the imagery search spans mixed providers — Copernicus Sentinel + USGS Landsat are free/public-domain, but commercial tiers are not. Surfaceable once the feed is restricted to the public-domain providers only. Low priority. No editorial barrier.

sources.upstream_source: Good-news RSS outlets (Good News Network, Positive.News, Optimist Daily, etc.), GDELT-geotagged
sources.surfaced_reason: HELD ON NEWS-FILTER GROUNDS (the news per-source filter, not topic sensitivity): the positive-events feed draws from good-news / optimism outlets (Optimist Daily, Reasons to be Cheerful, etc.) which are advocacy/editorial sources, not wire/agency fact-reporting. The same per-source filter that strips op-ed/advocacy from the general NEWS channel strips these. Surfaceable only if narrowed to wire/agency fact-reporting of positive events.

sources.upstream_source: Composite charitable-giving index (annual-report baselines + on-chain donation tracking)
sources.surfaced_reason: HELD AS DERIVED-COMPOSITE: the giving summary is a WorldMonitor-constructed composite INDEX (modelled from annual-report baselines + on-chain tracking), not a single raw observed feed — closer to an analytic product than a channel. Held pending a decomposition into its underlying free-licensed component facts. No editorial barrier.

HELD ON LICENSE GROUNDS (low priority): arXiv metadata is CC0 but per-paper full-text licensing is mixed, and the Hacker News / GitHub-trending sub-feeds are aggregator content. Surfaceable once narrowed to CC0/CC-BY metadata only. No editorial barrier.
